# Content-type test fixtures

A bank of small files — one per supported format — used by the
content-type test suite. All bytes are **CC0 / public domain**: every
file is generated by us (sine-wave audio, lavfi test patterns, hand-
written text, `magick`-rendered solid-colour images, pandoc / openpyxl
/ python-pptx / reportlab for office documents). No third-party
copyrightable input.

The bank is embedded into the test binary via `//go:embed` (see
`fixtures.go` next to this directory) so tests can drive every content
type from `embed.FS` without reading from disk.

## Inventory

| File                | Format                | Size     | Generator |
| ------------------- | --------------------- | -------- | --------- |
| `sample.md`         | Markdown + YAML FM    | ~500 B   | hand-written |
| `sample.html`       | HTML                  | ~290 B   | hand-written |
| `sample.xml`        | XML                   | ~175 B   | hand-written |
| `sample.json`       | JSON object           | ~115 B   | hand-written |
| `sample.csv`        | CSV (comma)           | ~90 B    | hand-written |
| `sample.tsv`        | TSV (tab)             | ~90 B    | hand-written |
| `sample.txt`        | Plain text            | ~85 B    | hand-written |
| `sample.svg`        | SVG                   | ~225 B   | hand-written |
| `sample.jpg`        | JPEG (16×16 solid)    | ~290 B   | `magick -size 16x16 xc:'#0d1117' sample.jpg` |
| `sample.png`        | PNG (16×16 solid)     | ~310 B   | `magick -size 16x16 xc:'#7d8590' sample.png` |
| `sample.gif`        | GIF (16×16 solid)     | ~55 B    | `magick -size 16x16 xc:'#3fb950' sample.gif` |
| `sample.webp`       | WebP (from PNG)       | ~50 B    | `cwebp -q 80 sample.png -o sample.webp` |
| `sample.tiff`       | TIFF (16×16 solid)    | ~1.8 KB  | `magick -size 16x16 xc:'#79c0ff' sample.tiff` |
| `sample.bmp`        | BMP3 (16×16 solid)    | ~820 B   | `magick -size 16x16 xc:'#ffa657' BMP3:sample.bmp` |
| `sample.heic`       | HEIC (from JPEG)      | ~465 B   | `heif-enc -q 50 sample.jpg -o sample.heic` |
| `sample.mp3`        | MP3, 1 s silence + ID3v2 | ~8.5 KB | `ffmpeg -f lavfi -i anullsrc=r=44100:cl=mono -t 1 -metadata title=… -id3v2_version 3 -c:a libmp3lame -b:a 64k …` |
| `sample.m4a`        | M4A (AAC), 1 s silence | ~1.3 KB | `ffmpeg -f lavfi -i anullsrc=r=44100:cl=mono -t 1 -metadata title=… -c:a aac -b:a 64k …` |
| `sample.flac`       | FLAC, 1 s silence     | ~8.5 KB  | `ffmpeg -f lavfi -i anullsrc=r=44100:cl=mono -t 1 -metadata title=… -c:a flac …` |
| `sample.ogg`        | OGG Vorbis, 1 s silence | ~5 KB  | `ffmpeg -f lavfi -i anullsrc=r=44100:cl=stereo -t 1 -metadata title=… -c:a vorbis -strict -2 …` |
| `sample.mp4`        | MP4 H.264, 1 s testsrc | ~7.2 KB | `ffmpeg -f lavfi -i testsrc=size=64x48:duration=1:rate=10 -c:v libx264 -preset ultrafast -pix_fmt yuv420p sample.mp4` |
| `sample.mov`        | MOV H.264             | ~7.1 KB  | same as MP4, `-f mov` |
| `sample.mkv`        | Matroska H.264        | ~7 KB    | same as MP4, `-f matroska` |
| `sample.webm`       | WebM VP9              | ~3.7 KB  | `ffmpeg -f lavfi -i testsrc=… -c:v libvpx-vp9 -b:v 50k -f webm …` |
| `sample.avi`        | AVI MPEG-4            | ~9.8 KB  | `ffmpeg -f lavfi -i testsrc=… -c:v mpeg4 -f avi …` |
| `sample.pdf`        | PDF                   | ~1.5 KB  | reportlab via `uvx --with reportlab python3 …` |
| `sample.epub`       | EPUB                  | ~5.1 KB  | `pandoc -t epub` from a stub markdown |
| `sample.docx`       | OOXML DOCX            | ~10.5 KB | `pandoc -t docx` |
| `sample.xlsx`       | OOXML XLSX            | ~5 KB    | openpyxl via `uvx --with openpyxl python3 …` |
| `sample.pptx`       | OOXML PPTX            | ~28 KB   | python-pptx via `uvx --with python-pptx python3 …` |
| `sample.odt`        | OpenDocument          | ~6.9 KB  | `pandoc -t odt` |

## Regeneration

The full bank can be regenerated by running `make_fixtures.sh` in this
directory. The script lists every command that produced each file. It
needs `ffmpeg`, `pandoc`, `magick` (ImageMagick 7), `cwebp`, `heif-enc`,
and `uv` / `uvx` available on `PATH`. CI **does not** run the script —
fixtures are committed binaries.

## Licence

All files in this directory are released into the public domain under
[CC0 1.0](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/). They
contain no copyrightable expression — solid colours, generated test
patterns, silence, and trivially-original prose written for the test
suite.
